rules.name: no-direct-pytest-python-airflow-on-host
rules.evidence: Never run pytest, python, or airflow commands directly on the host — always use breeze.

rules.name: ruff-format-after-python-edit
rules.evidence: Always format and check Python files with ruff immediately after writing or editing them

rules.name: ruff-format-after-python-write
rules.evidence: Do this for every Python file you create or modify, before moving on to the next step.

rules.name: no-assert-in-production-code
rules.evidence: No `assert` in production code.

rules.name: no-time-time-for-durations
rules.evidence: `time.monotonic()` for durations, not `time.time()`.

rules.name: no-session-commit-in-airflow-core
rules.evidence: functions with a `session` parameter must not call `session.commit()`

rules.name: no-new-raise-airflow-exception-edit
rules.evidence: Never add new direct `raise AirflowException(...)` usages

rules.name: no-new-raise-airflow-exception-write
rules.evidence: Never add new direct `raise AirflowException(...)` usages

rules.name: translate-exceptions-to-httpexception
rules.evidence: re-raise as `HTTPException` with the right status (`404` for not-found, `400` for invalid input)

rules.name: batch-bulk-delete-update-in-scheduler
rules.evidence: never issue a single unbounded bulk write against a user-driven table

rules.name: apache-license-header-new-files
rules.evidence: Apache License header on all new files (prek enforces this).

rules.name: keep-selective-checks-doc-in-sync
rules.evidence: update `dev/breeze/doc/ci/04_selective_checks.md` in the same PR

rules.name: no-newsfragment-for-providers-or-airflow-ctl
rules.evidence: Never add newsfragments for `providers/` or `airflow-ctl/`

rules.name: newsfragment-only-if-user-facing
rules.evidence: Golden rule: never create a newsfragment unless you are certain the change is user-facing

rules.name: no-unittest-testcase
rules.evidence: Use pytest patterns, not `unittest.TestCase`.

rules.name: prefer-autospec-mocking
rules.evidence: Use `spec`/`autospec` when mocking.

rules.name: prefer-mock-patch-decorator
rules.evidence: Prefer `@mock.patch` decorators over `with mock.patch(...)` context managers for patching.

rules.name: no-datetime-now-in-tests
rules.evidence: Do not use `datetime.now()`

rules.name: no-caplog-in-tests
rules.evidence: Do not use `caplog` in tests, prefer checking logic and not log output.

rules.name: commit-message-style-user-impact
rules.evidence: Write commit messages focused on user impact, not implementation details

rules.name: no-conventional-commit-prefix
rules.evidence: Airflow does not use Conventional Commits (`feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:` …)

rules.name: run-prek-install-before-commit
rules.evidence: Always run `prek install` before committing any code.

rules.name: no-self-co-authored-by
rules.evidence: NEVER add Co-Authored-By with yourself as co-author of the commit.

rules.name: verify-remotes-before-push-or-pr
rules.evidence: Before running any remote-based command, run `git remote -v` and verify the names match this convention.

rules.name: no-destructive-push-to-upstream-or-main
rules.evidence: Never push directly to `upstream` (and never push directly to `main` on either remote).

rules.name: self-review-checklist-before-pr
rules.evidence: perform a self-review of your changes following the Gen-AI review guidelines

rules.name: rebase-before-push
rules.evidence: Before pushing, always rebase your branch onto the latest target branch

rules.name: pr-title-format
rules.evidence: keep it short (under 70 chars), in the imperative mood, and focused on user impact

rules.name: pr-body-ai-disclosure
rules.evidence: the body pre-filled (including the generative AI disclosure already checked)

rules.name: no-issue-when-fix-is-imminent
rules.evidence: do not file a GitHub issue first. Go straight to the PR.

rules.name: tracking-issue-for-deferred-work
rules.evidence: the deferred work must be captured in a GitHub tracking issue

rules.name: no-vague-forward-looking-language
rules.pattern: (?=[\s\S]*gh\s+(pr|issue)\s+(create|comment))(?=[\s\S]*(will open a tracking issue|to be filed later))
rules.evidence: Do not write vague forward-looking phrases like "will open a tracking issue" or "to be filed later"

rules.name: tracking-issue-full-url-in-comment
rules.evidence: Use the **full issue URL**, not bare `#NNNNN`

rules.name: attribution-footer-on-github-posts
rules.evidence: must end with an attribution footer

rules.name: no-tagging-individuals
rules.evidence: AI agents MUST NOT mention or tag individual contributors

rules.name: no-hand-edit-uv-lock
rules.evidence: Edit generated files by hand when a generation workflow exists.

rules.name: no-commit-secrets
rules.evidence: Commit secrets, credentials, or tokens.

rules.name: no-destructive-git-ops-unless-requested
rules.evidence: Use destructive git operations unless explicitly requested.

rules.name: no-hand-edit-magpie-snapshot
rules.evidence: never edit the snapshot directly

rules.name: ask-before-new-dependency
rules.evidence: New dependencies with broad impact.

rules.name: ask-before-destructive-migration
rules.evidence: Destructive data or migration changes.

uncompilable: Dag/DAG capitalization convention in prose (title-case 'Dag' vs literal code tokens) — general writing style, not action-bindable
One-time environment setup steps (install prek, enable commit hooks initially, install breeze shim) — bootstrap steps, not per-action triggers
Commands reference table (test/lint/build/type-check commands) — usage catalog listing what you can run, no attached obligation
Reactive troubleshooting tips ('if uv tests fail with missing deps, use breeze', 'if Docker networking fails, run docker network prune') — reactive fixes after failure, not preemptive action triggers
Repository structure documentation (directory purposes) — pure documentation, not a rule
Architecture Boundaries (Scheduler never runs user code, Workers never access metadata DB directly, etc.) — descriptive system design for code review context, not a tool-bound agent action

Security Model vulnerability-classification guidance (distinguishing real vulnerabilities from known limitations vs hardening opportunities) — reasoning guidance for triage, not tied to a specific tool call
Comment sparingly / when comments are warranted — general coding style at large
Imports at top of file, with exceptions for circular imports/lazy loading/TYPE_CHECKING — general style guidance
Guard heavy type-only imports with TYPE_CHECKING in multi-process code paths — judgment-heavy, no reliable regex trigger
Name functions/methods with action verbs, avoid noun-only names — general naming style
Target exactly 100% coverage of what the PR changes, no more no less — judgment-based test-scope guidance
Use @pytest.mark.parametrize to consolidate similar tests — style/judgment guidance
Use @pytest.mark.db_test for tests requiring database access — requires semantic judgment about DB access, no reliable trigger
Test location mirrors source path convention (airflow/cli/cli_parser.py → tests/cli/test_cli_parser.py) — descriptive convention, not phrased as an obligation
Place temporary scripts under dev/ — no trigger reliably distinguishes 'temporary script' writes from ordinary source writes
Put any generated files (PR reviews, reports, scratch output) under files/ — same issue, no trigger separates generated output from normal source writes
Check for existing PRs before starting work on an issue — happens before any tool call, not bindable to a specific action
Prefer the magpie-pr-management-code-review skill over an ad-hoc review pass — a skill/workflow choice, not a tool-call trigger
Ask first before large cross-package refactors — too vague to bind to any file or command pattern
Use conf_vars for Airflow config overrides, as decorator when fixed vs context manager when varying via parametrize — nuanced conditional style guidance
